summary: Set 048 Session 5 is the UAT + close-out session of the audit-locked Lightweight-tier parity arc. The session's distinct deliverable beyond the UAT artifact and the dual version bumps is the UAT-discovered Critical bare-import bug fix: running the smoke test on the route()/verify() short-circuit under simulated pip-install sys.path surfaced that the entire --no-router mode shipped in S2 was a silent no-op for pip-installed consumers — 5 production-code sites used bare imports of the new Set 048 modules (from runtime_mode import …) that only resolved under the test conftest.py's sys.path shim. route() and verify() exploded outright in pip-install mode; start_session.main() and close_session.run() wrapped the bare import in try/except: pass, silently no-op'ing --no-router for the entire production CLI surface. The original S2 Round-A verifier had flagged this as Major #2 and the dismissal as false-positive turned out to be wrong; S5 fixes it (5 production sites switched to relative imports, conftest module-aliasing block preserves the bare-import test convention) and locks the invariant out with a static-analysis test on an ast.walk of ai_router/**/*.py. 42-item UAT checklist authored in the Set-045 schema (Dabbler UAT-Checklist-Editor compatible) covering every audit-locked §3.x deliverable + L1-L5 directives + P1 parity invariants; 31 of 45 final items verified complete by the AI orchestrator programmatically; 14 flagged Manual operator UAT post-publish for live-VS-Code surfaces. Dual version bumps: pyproject.toml 0.9.0 -> 0.10.0; package.json 0.22.0 -> 0.23.0. CHANGELOG.md + CLAUDE.md walks. .vsix build sanity-check succeeded (881.47 KB, 23 files). Cross-provider verification (gpt-5-4, tier 3, $0.1806 routed) returned pass_with_findings with 3 findings — Major #1 (static-analysis guard narrower than docstring) rewritten on ast.walk + 9 scanner self-tests; Minor #2 (conftest alias import-order sensitive) addressed via defensive check; Minor #3 (UAT checklist missing P1 traceability) addressed via 3 new P1-parity items. 1020 Python pass + 1 skip (was 1010 + 1; 0 regressions). Cumulative Set 048 routed spend $0.94 of $10 NTE (9.4%); Set 048 closes within budget across all 5 sessions. Pattern lesson recorded: dismissing verifier findings as false positives without empirically reproducing them is itself a risk pattern — S2's dismissed Major #2 was the Critical bug S5 had to fix.

reason.specifics: Set 048 closes. Per project_set_049_stubbed.md, Set 049 (orchestrator-coordination-removal) is the next stubbed set — full rip-out of check-out/check-in code + orchestrator-block simplification + Session Set Explorer cleanup. The Set 049 stub awaits its own S1 audit pass before implementation begins. Before Set 049 starts, Set 048's publishes must ship: PyPI 0.10.0 (push tag v0.10.0) and Marketplace 0.23.0 (push tag vsix-v0.23.0) per reference_publish_via_github_actions. Both publishes are operator-initiated. The Set 047 Marketplace 0.22.0 publish that was held failed on 2026-05-26 with /_apis/gallery timeouts; Set 048's 0.23.0 supersedes it (Marketplace consumers only see the latest artifact, no separate retry needed).
